source_text:
Heavy Rain Alert Issued In Parts Of Tamil Nadu

Chennai, July 3: Heavy rain in parts of Tamil Nadu has prompted district administrations to issue safety advisories for residents living in low-lying and flood-prone areas.

Officials said rainfall was reported across several coastal and interior districts, leading to waterlogging on some roads and traffic disruption during peak hours. Disaster response teams have been placed on standby, while local bodies have been instructed to keep pumps, generators and emergency vehicles ready.

The weather department said more rain is likely over the next two days due to a weather system over the Bay of Bengal. Fishermen have been advised not to venture into rough sea conditions until further notice.

In urban areas, residents have been asked to avoid driving through flooded roads and report fallen trees, open electric cables and overflowing drains to local authorities. Schools in some affected areas may shift to online classes if the rain continues.

Officials said reservoirs and major water bodies are being monitored, but there is no immediate cause for panic. They added that precautionary measures are being taken to avoid last-minute emergency responses.

Residents have been advised to follow official updates and avoid spreading unverified weather alerts on social media.
